title: arthromeris wallichiana
intro: Arthromeris wallichiana, also known as the Himalayan maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that is native to the Himalayan region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an anti-arthritic agent. In herbal preparations, Arthromeris wallichiana is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or as a dried powder, and it is commonly consumed as a tea or added to other herbal remedies.
